title: Spokeo, Inc. v. Robins (2016) — Article III Standing and FCRA

timeline.event: Supreme Court decides Spokeo (vacates and remands Ninth Circuit)

evidence_artifacts: Data lineage documentation for published attributes
Accuracy testing and QA sampling records
Consumer dispute logs and resolution SLAs
Legal review records for marketing claims about accuracy
engineering_takeaways: Statutory violations alone may not establish Article III injury; concrete harm still matters in federal court.
Accuracy-sensitive products need procedural rigor comparable to legal duties for consumer reporting contexts.
